question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of unique entity-lookup requests when Cache.MaxSize is set to 0 (zero-value, no validation enforced), causing the LRU cache in NewBlockAPIService to be initialized with capacity 0 and every entity lookup to bypass caching, forcing repeated DB queries on every /block request and degrading service for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user trigger cache thrashing by sending a high volume of /block requests referencing distinct entity IDs when Cache.MaxSize is set to a very small positive integer (e.g., 1), causing constant LRU eviction and forcing the server to re-query the database for every operation, degrading response times for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a lower-bound validation on Cache.MaxSize by sending requests that populate the entity cache with a large number of distinct int64 entity IDs, causing legitimate cached entries to be evicted and forcing other users' requests to incur full database round-trips?
